steps.title: Identify the angle pair formed at line q
steps.content: At the intersection of transversal $t$ with line $q$, the angles marked $x^\circ$ and $142^\circ$ are adjacent angles that together form a straight line. Angles that form a linear pair are supplementary.

steps.title: Apply the linear pair property and eliminate the distractors
steps.content:
Two adjacent angles on a straight line must sum to $180^\circ$. Therefore $x + 142 = 180$.
  • Choice A — 52: this would be the value of $x$ alone ($180 - 142$), not $x + 142$. ✗
  • Choice B — 90: a linear pair sums to $180^\circ$, not $90^\circ$ (that would be a right angle). ✗
  • Choice C — 142: this would imply $x = 0$, which contradicts the linear-pair sum. ✗
  • Choice D — 180: matches the linear-pair sum $x + 142 = 180$. ✓
